title: Top 25 Prompt Engineering Voices of 2026
subtitle: The most influential prompt engineers, AI engineers, and educators shaping how professionals work with AI
methodology: Ranked by cross-list appearances, community impact, published work, and relevance to prompt engineering and AI engineering. Sources include DAIR.AI, Anthropic team tracking, OpenAI ecosystem, DeepLearning.AI, Learn Prompting, LangChain community, and independent analysis. We evaluated 50+ candidates and filtered for people who actively advance how professionals interact with AI systems.

voices.name: Riley Goodside
voices.title: Staff Prompt Engineer
voices.bio: The original prompt engineer and first person to hold the 'Staff Prompt Engineer' title at Scale AI. His early explorations of GPT-3 prompt injection, jailbreaking, and creative prompting on Twitter defined the field before it had a name. Now at Google DeepMind. Every prompt engineering technique traces some lineage back to his experiments.

voices.name: Simon Willison
voices.title: Creator & Developer
voices.bio: Creator of Datasette and the LLM command-line tool. Django co-creator. Built 150+ HTML tools for working with LLMs. His blog at simonwillison.net is the most comprehensive technical resource on working with LLMs in practice. Covers prompt engineering, tool use, RAG, and AI safety with a depth and rigor no one else matches.

voices.name: Elvis Saravia
voices.title: Co-Founder
voices.bio: Created the Prompt Engineering Guide (promptingguide.ai), the most-starred prompt engineering resource on GitHub (50K+ stars) that has reached 3M+ learners. Founded DAIR.AI. Former Meta AI / Papers with Code. Teaches advanced PE courses on Maven. The curriculum thousands of practitioners learned prompt engineering from.

voices.name: Amanda Askell
voices.title: Head of Alignment Finetuning
voices.bio: Philosopher-turned-AI-researcher who leads Claude's personality alignment at Anthropic. Key contributor to Constitutional AI. Known as 'Anthropic's resident philosopher' for prompt engineering. PhD from NYU, former OpenAI. Her work literally shapes how Claude responds to prompts at the model level.

voices.name: Lilian Weng
voices.title: Head of Safety Systems
voices.bio: Head of Safety Systems at OpenAI. Her blog (lilianweng.github.io) is the most technically rigorous resource on LLM agents, prompt engineering patterns, and AI systems design. Posts like 'Prompt Engineering' and 'LLM Powered Autonomous Agents' are canonical references cited in research papers and production systems alike.

voices.name: Andrej Karpathy
voices.title: Founder & Educator
voices.bio: Former Director of AI at Tesla and founding member of OpenAI. Now building Eureka Labs for AI education. His YouTube lectures on neural networks and LLMs have millions of views. 'Let's build GPT from scratch' is the single best resource for understanding how the models behind prompt engineering actually work.

voices.name: Sander Schulhoff
voices.title: CEO
voices.bio: Created Learn Prompting (learnprompting.org), the first prompt engineering guide online, before ChatGPT launched. Runs HackAPrompt, the world's largest AI red-teaming competition (600K+ prompts). Lead author of 'The Prompt Report,' a 76-page systematic review with OpenAI, Microsoft, Google, Princeton, and Stanford researchers.

voices.name: Harrison Chase
voices.title: CEO & Co-Founder
voices.bio: Built LangChain, the most popular framework for building LLM applications, plus LangGraph and LangSmith. Harvard stats/CS graduate. Prompt chaining and agent orchestration pioneer. His abstractions for prompt templates, chains, and agents defined how the industry structures prompt-driven applications.

voices.name: Ethan Mollick
voices.title: Professor & Author
voices.bio: TIME's Most Influential in AI. Co-Director of Generative AI Labs at Wharton. NYT bestselling author of 'Co-Intelligence.' His Substack 'One Useful Thing' is where non-technical professionals learn how to prompt effectively. Advocates 'good enough prompting,' showing that conversation skills matter more than engineering syntax.

voices.name: Zack Witten
voices.title: Senior Prompt Engineer
voices.bio: Known as the 'Prompt Doctor' at Anthropic. Leads hands-on Claude prompt workshops at conferences. MS in NLP from Northwestern. His practical demonstrations of advanced prompting techniques for Claude give practitioners the closest thing to an official masterclass from the model maker.

voices.name: Swyx (Shawn Wang)
voices.title: Founder
voices.bio: Runs the Latent Space podcast and newsletter covering AI engineering. Coined 'AI Engineer' as a role category and curates the AI Engineer Summit. Former devtools lead at AWS, Netlify, Temporal, Airbyte. His content on building AI applications bridges the gap between prompt engineering and software engineering.

voices.name: Logan Kilpatrick
voices.title: Product Lead
voices.bio: First developer relations hire at OpenAI (pre-ChatGPT). Now heads product for Google AI Studio / Gemini API. The key bridge between AI researchers and the developer community. His transition from OpenAI to Google gives him a unique cross-platform perspective on prompt engineering best practices.

voices.name: Andrew Ng
voices.title: Founder
voices.bio: 3M+ LinkedIn followers. Co-created the landmark 'ChatGPT Prompt Engineering for Developers' free course with OpenAI (300K+ signups in one week). Co-founder of Coursera. Former Chief Scientist at Baidu. The person who made prompt engineering accessible to developers at massive scale.

voices.name: Jeremy Howard
voices.title: Co-Founder
voices.bio: Co-founded fast.ai. Developed ULMFiT, the transfer learning technique underlying all modern LLMs. Former President and Chief Scientist at Kaggle. Now building Answer.AI to make AI tools work for everyday users. His practical, code-first education philosophy shaped how a generation learned to work with AI.

voices.name: Rome Thorndike
voices.title: Founder
voices.bio: Built PE Collective into a community of 1,300+ prompt engineers and AI practitioners. Runs AI News Digest newsletter with 3,000+ subscribers. Created the job board and salary data that gave the prompt engineering profession its first career intelligence platform.

voices.name: Alex Albert
voices.title: Head of Developer Relations
voices.bio: Started as a prompt engineer at Anthropic and created widely shared prompting tips content. Now leads all developer relations for Claude. His trajectory from prompt engineer to DevRel lead shows how prompt engineering skills translate into technical leadership at AI companies.

voices.name: Matt Shumer
voices.title: CEO
voices.bio: Created gpt-prompt-engineer (15K+ GitHub stars) for automatic prompt optimization. Pioneered the prompt expansion technique. Built HyperWrite, one of the first AI writing assistants built on advanced prompting. His open-source tools push the boundaries of what's possible with careful prompt design.

voices.name: Jason Liu
voices.title: Creator
voices.bio: Created Instructor, the most popular library for structured output from LLMs. Solved one of the biggest practical problems in prompt engineering: making LLMs return reliable, typed data instead of free text. Active teacher on prompt design patterns that work in production.

voices.name: Chip Huyen
voices.title: Author & ML Engineer
voices.bio: Author of 'Designing Machine Learning Systems.' Her writing on building production AI systems, including prompt management, evaluation, and deployment, provides the systems-level thinking prompt engineers need as they move from experimentation to production.

voices.name: Marily Nika
voices.title: Gen AI Product Lead
voices.bio: Fortune 40 Under 40. Runs the #1 AI Product Management Bootcamp on Maven (20K+ students). PhD in ML, 12+ years at Google and Meta. Bridges prompt engineering and product management, showing how PE skills translate into building AI-powered products.

voices.name: Philipp Schmid
voices.title: Tech Lead
voices.bio: Tech lead at Hugging Face. His tutorials on fine-tuning, prompt engineering for open-source models, and deploying LLMs have become the standard reference for practitioners working outside the OpenAI ecosystem. Essential voice for the open-source PE community.

voices.name: Rowan Cheung
voices.title: Creator
voices.bio: Forbes 30 Under 30 (2026). 2M+ newsletter subscribers. His daily AI digest is how many professionals first discover prompt engineering concepts and stay current on what's possible. The scale of his audience makes him one of the most effective popularizers of PE techniques.

voices.name: Ruben Hassid
voices.title: AI Content Creator
voices.bio: 280K LinkedIn followers. Ranked 2nd World Content Creator on LinkedIn by Favikon. Specializes in actionable prompt templates for scaling content with AI. His practical, hands-on approach helps professionals implement prompt engineering in their daily workflows immediately.

voices.name: Cassie Kozyrkov
voices.title: CEO
voices.bio: Former Chief Decision Scientist at Google. 1M LinkedIn followers. LinkedIn Top Voice 5 years running. Her content on AI literacy and decision-making with AI tools shapes how professionals think about when and how to use prompting vs. other approaches. The voice of pragmatism in a hype-heavy field.

voices.name: Dan Shipper
voices.title: CEO & Editor
voices.bio: Runs Every, an AI-native media company with 15 people writing virtually zero code. Backed by Reid Hoffman. His 'Chain of Thought' column documents how he uses prompting to build products, write, and think. One of the best examples of prompt engineering deeply integrated into a creative and business workflow.
